To create a new function with the same behavior as the original
To prevent a function from being invoked

questions.text: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN in SQL?
options: INNER JOIN returns all rows, while LEFT JOIN returns only matching rows
INNER JOIN returns only matching rows, while LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table

WHERE is used for aggregating data, while HAVING is used for filtering data
questions.correctAnswer: WHERE is used for filtering individual rows, while HAVING is used for filtering grouped data
